question: Will Trump visit China by May 31?
description:
If U.S. President Donald Trump visits China by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purpose of this market, a "visit" is defined as Trump physically entering the terrestrial or maritime territory of the listed country. Whether or not Trump enters the country's airspace during the timeframe of this market will have no bearing on a positive resolution.

The primary resolution source for this information will be official information from government of the United States of America, official information from Trump or released by his verified social media accounts (e.g. https://twitter.com/POTUS), however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: Will Trump visit China by...?
events.description:
If U.S. President Donald Trump visits China by October 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purpose of this market, a "visit" is defined as Trump physically entering the terrestrial or maritime territory of the listed country. Whether or not Trump enters the country's airspace during the timeframe of this market will have no bearing on a positive resolution.

The primary resolution source for this information will be official information from government of the United States of America, official information from Trump or released by his verified social media accounts (e.g. https://twitter.com/POTUS), however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: President-elect Trump's lack of announced travel plans to China remains the primary driver of low trader consensus on an imminent visit, amid heightened US-China tensions over trade tariffs, Taiwan, and technology restrictions. Recent cabinet nominations, including China hawks like Marco Rubio for Secretary of State, signal a confrontational stance rather than diplomatic outreach, contrasting with his first-term 2017 Mar-a-Lago summit. No official White House or transition team statements confirm a trip before inauguration on January 20, 2025, though bilateral calls with Xi Jinping could shift dynamics. Traders watch for G20 summits or tariff escalation responses as potential catalysts, with historical precedents showing early-term visits rare amid disputes.

question: FC Augsburg leading at halftime?
description: In the upcoming game, scheduled for March 22, 2026 If FC Augsburg wins within the first 45 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time, this market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No". This market refers only to the outcome within the first 45 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 24 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: FC Augsburg vs. VfB Stuttgart - Halftime Result
events.description: In the upcoming Bundesliga game between FC Augsburg and VfB Stuttgart, scheduled for March 22, 2026 at 2:30 PM ET: This event contains halftime result markets for home, draw, and away outcomes within the first 45 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects razor-thin halftime equilibrium for FC Augsburg versus VfB Stuttgart, driven by Augsburg's stout home first-half defenses—conceding just once in their last four Bundesliga home openers—and Stuttgart's road caution despite their league-leading attack. No major injuries disrupt either lineup per official reports, with full-strength squads expected for Saturday's clash. Recent form underscores the stalemate: Augsburg's three halftime draws in six games, Stuttgart's two away HT leads but vulnerability to counters. Head-to-heads average under one first-half goal, reinforcing crowd wisdom that early probes yield parity over dominance. Schedule rest favors neither, amplifying uncertainty in this mid-table scrap.
